question: Find the New York counties (excluding NYC boroughs) having average adult mental health Medicaid paid claims between $75 million and $95 million across the time window starting with the NYC year (within 2017-2022) with the highest average annual PM2.5 mean and ending with the year (within 2018-2019) with the lowest total ridership for Bloomington Transit bus routes. According to the NY Utility Energy Registry, which of these counties had the lower average total natural gas consumption across those same years. That county was named after an English county. Tell me what was the medieval name of the county town of that English county? Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Filtering to Name = "Fine particles (PM 2.5)", Measure = "Mean", and Time Period containing "Annual Average" for 2017-2022, the highest average Data Value is 7.74113475177305 in 2017.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2017-2022 in New York City has the highest average annual PM2.5 mean? (Filter Name == "Fine particles (PM 2.5)", Measure == "Mean", Time Period contains "Annual Average"; average Data Value by year and select the maximum.)

2.fact: Summing riders by year for 2018-2019, the minimum total is 3106507 in 2018.
2.subquestion: Which year from 2018-2019 has the lowest total ridership for Bloomington Transit bus routes? (Use ts to derive year; sum riders by year and select the minimum.)

3.fact: In 2017, New York counties' adult mental health Medicaid paid claims included: Suffolk County ($92,420,995), Erie County ($70,519,223), Westchester ($66,346,132), Nassau ($62,927,542), and Monroe ($48,278,139).
3.subquestion: What were the adult mental health Medicaid paid claims for New York counties in <node_1 answer>?

4.fact: In 2018, New York counties' adult mental health Medicaid paid claims included: Suffolk County ($87,249,830), Erie County ($81,139,374), Westchester ($67,149,496), Nassau ($63,138,172), and Monroe ($51,731,436).
4.subquestion: What were the adult mental health Medicaid paid claims for New York counties in <node_2 answer>?

5.fact: In 2017, total natural gas consumption (Total Consumption (T), Therms) by county included: Erie County (621,451,910.24), Suffolk County (537,382,172), Monroe County (441,616,268.322), Westchester County (378,456,122).
5.subquestion: What was the total natural gas consumption (Total Consumption (T), Therms; data_class=natural_gas) for Erie and Suffolk counties (from <aggregation of nodes 3-4: counties with avg $75M-$95M>) in <node_1 answer>?

6.fact: In 2018, total natural gas consumption (Total Consumption (T), Therms) by county included: Erie County (712,460,330.24), Suffolk County (556,444,750), Monroe County (548,282,992.958), Westchester County (408,624,973).
6.subquestion: What was the total natural gas consumption (Total Consumption (T), Therms; data_class=natural_gas) for Erie and Suffolk counties (from <aggregation of nodes 3-4: counties with avg $75M-$95M>) in <node_2 answer>?

7.fact: Suffolk County was named after the county of Suffolk in England, the origin of its earliest European settlers.
7.subquestion: After which English county is <aggregation of nodes 5-6: Suffolk, which has lower average energy (546,913,461 < 666,956,120)> named?

8.fact: Ipswich is a port town and borough in Suffolk, England. It is the county town and largest settlement in Suffolk, followed by Lowestoft and Bury St Edmunds.
8.subquestion: What is the county town of <node 7: Suffolk, England>?

9.fact: Ipswich was first recorded during the medieval period as Gippeswic. The town has also been recorded as Gyppewicus and Yppswyche.
9.subquestion: What was the medieval name of <node 8: Ipswich>?

reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=2, data.gov year derivation - nodes 1-2):
Node 1: Year with highest NYC PM2.5 mean (2017-2022) -> 2017
Node 2: Year with lowest Bloomington Transit ridership (2018-2019) -> 2018
HOP 2 (d=2, data.gov AGGREGATION - nodes 3-4):
Node 3: County Mental Health Profiles 2017, Adult paid claims -> {Suffolk: $92.4M, Erie: $70.5M, ...}
Node 4: County Mental Health Profiles 2018, Adult paid claims -> {Suffolk: $87.2M, Erie: $81.1M, ...}
Aggregation: Average(2017, 2018) for each county
Filter: Counties with avg $75M-$95M (excluding NYC) -> {Erie, Suffolk}
Note: Both nodes NECESSARY to compute the average; neither alone suffices
HOP 3 (d=2, data.gov AGGREGATION - nodes 5-6 comparing counties' natural gas consumption):
Node 5: Utility Energy Registry 2017 (Total Consumption (T), Therms; data_class=natural_gas) -> Erie: 621,451,910.24, Suffolk: 537,382,172
Node 6: Utility Energy Registry 2018 (Total Consumption (T), Therms; data_class=natural_gas) -> Erie: 712,460,330.24, Suffolk: 556,444,750
Aggregation: Average(2017, 2018)
Result: Suffolk has lower average (546,913,461 < 666,956,120.24) -> Suffolk wins
Note: Both nodes NECESSARY to compute the average; neither alone suffices
HOP 4 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 7 identifies English namesake):
Node 7: Suffolk County, NY Wikipedia -> named after Suffolk, England
HOP 5 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 8 depends on node 7):
Node 8: Ipswich Wikipedia -> Ipswich is the county town of Suffolk, England
HOP 6 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 9 depends on node 8):
Node 9: Ipswich Wikipedia -> medieval name was Gippeswic
Final answer: Gippeswic
